skill.name: instagram-automation
skill.description: Automate Instagram tasks via Rube MCP (Composio): create posts, carousels, manage media, get insights, and publishing limits. Always search tools first for current schemas.
skill.summary: Guide Instagram publishing, media retrieval, insights, and limits through Rube MCP.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance alerts are false positives from Markdown tool names and prose in SKILL.md. The real risk is medium because the skill instructs users to connect a third-party MCP endpoint and perform OAuth-scoped Instagram publishing and analytics actions.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party MCP Endpoint and OAuth Connection
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE for network risk. The skill instructs users to add https://rube.app/mcp as an MCP server and connect Instagram through RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS. This is a legitimate integration path, but it delegates account access to an external service and requires user trust in that endpoint.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The external endpoint and OAuth connection workflow are explicit in the skill text. The finding is medium, not high, because the text describes normal setup rather than credential theft or covert exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: OAuth-Scoped Instagram Publishing Actions
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE for operational risk. The skill documents workflows that create media containers and publish Instagram posts or carousels for a connected Business or Creator account. Misuse or mistaken prompts could publish public content, so operators should require explicit user confirmation before publishing.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The publishing tool sequences are directly listed in SKILL.md. The risk depends on granted OAuth permissions and user intent, so it is elevated but not evidence of malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: External Command Alerts Are Markdown Tool References
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The repeated Ruby or shell backtick detections are Markdown inline code spans naming Rube and Instagram MCP tools, parameters, or workflow steps. No executable shell, Ruby backtick execution, or command construction is present in the reviewed SKILL.md content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged syntax appears in prose and Markdown lists, not in scripts or executable files. The repository scan contains only SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alert Is a False Positive
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The alert points to the YAML description line, which describes Instagram automation through Rube MCP. No hashing, encryption, cryptographic API, or weak algorithm use appears at that location.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is metadata text, not code. I found no evidence of cryptographic behavior in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Alerts Are Workflow Text
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The flagged lines describe Instagram publishing limits, response IDs, status polling, and a quick reference table of Instagram MCP tool names. They do not inspect the host system or collect local environment details.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context is Instagram API workflow documentation. No host reconnaissance commands or local system queries are present.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Service Configuration
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill requires configuring an external MCP server at https://rube.app/mcp. This is expected for the integration, but it means actions and account authorization depend on a third-party service.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The endpoint is explicitly documented. The risk is architectural rather than evidence of malicious code.

content.value_statement: Instagram Graph API workflows require several ordered calls and careful status checks. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a practical Rube MCP guide for posts, carousels, media, insights, and publishing limits.
actual_capabilities: Guides setup for Rube MCP and Instagram OAuth connection checks.
Lists tool sequences for publishing single image or video posts.
Explains carousel container creation with two to ten media items.
Documents media lookup, post insights, account insights, and comments workflows.
Highlights publishing limits, media URL requirements, and status polling steps.
limitations: Requires Rube MCP and an active Instagram connection.
Works only with Instagram Business or Creator accounts.
Requires public HTTPS media URLs for publishing workflows.
Does not provide custom code, scheduling logic, or content moderation.

use_cases.title: Publish Campaign Posts
use_cases.description: Prepare and publish approved image or video posts through the documented Instagram tool sequence.

use_cases.title: Create Product Carousels
use_cases.description: Build carousel posts by creating child containers, checking processing status, and publishing the final carousel.

use_cases.title: Review Account Performance
use_cases.description: Retrieve media details, comments, and insights for Business or Creator account reporting.

prompt_templates.title: Check Connection
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Instagram automation skill to verify that Rube MCP is available and my Instagram connection is active. Do not publish anything.

prompt_templates.title: Publish Single Post
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Instagram automation workflow to publish one approved image post with this public image URL and caption. Confirm the account and publishing limit first.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Carousel
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an Instagram carousel workflow for these approved public media URLs. Check each container status before publishing the carousel.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Recent Media
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Instagram media and insights workflows to summarize recent post performance, comments, and account-level metrics for the selected period.

output_examples.input: Check whether I can publish today before creating a post.
output_examples.output: The assistant verifies the active Instagram connection, retrieves the account ID, checks the content publishing limit, and reports whether publishing should proceed.

output_examples.input: Publish a four-image carousel with this approved caption.
output_examples.output: The assistant creates one container per public media URL, waits for processing, creates the carousel container, requests confirmation, and publishes it.

output_examples.input: Show insights for my last campaign post.
output_examples.output: The assistant lists recent media, selects the requested post, retrieves available metrics, and summarizes reach, impressions, engagement, and data delays.

best_practices: Confirm the account, caption, media URLs, and publishing intent before any publish action.
Call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first because MCP tool schemas can change.
Check container status and publishing limits before final publication.
anti_patterns: Do not publish from private, expired, or authenticated media URLs.
Do not assume personal Instagram accounts can use these workflows.
Do not skip status polling for videos or carousel children.

faq.question: Does this skill publish directly to Instagram?
faq.answer: It documents Rube MCP tool workflows that can publish after the user grants access and confirms the action.

faq.question: What account type is required?
faq.answer: An Instagram Business or Creator account connected to a Facebook Page is required.

faq.question: Can it use local image files?
faq.answer: No. Publishing workflows require public HTTPS image or video URLs.

faq.question: Can it create carousel posts?
faq.answer: Yes. It describes creating child media containers, creating the carousel container, checking status, and publishing.

faq.question: Does it handle Instagram insights?
faq.answer: Yes. It lists workflows for post insights, account insights, media details, comments, and carousel children.

faq.question: Is this safe to use?
faq.answer: Use caution. The skill depends on a third-party MCP endpoint and OAuth permissions for a connected Instagram account.
